Search results: 



--- SOURCE 1: Public perception and knowledge on nanotechnology ---
URL: https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2452074819301107

SUMMARY:
by IA Joubert · 2020 · Cited by 70 — The majority of respondents have a positive/neutral attitude towards nanotechnology. The attitude towards nanotechnology was not affected by age or education.



--------------------------------------------------------------------------------


--- SOURCE 2: Current research on public perceptions of nanotechnology ---
URL: https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC3167657/

SUMMARY:
by J Besley · 2010 · Cited by 87 — This review explores research on public perceptions of nanotechnology. It highlights a recurring emphasis on some researchers' expectations.



--------------------------------------------------------------------------------


--- SOURCE 3: Evaluation of public perceptions on nanotechnology ... ---
URL: https://academic.oup.com/spp/article/51/6/1015/7716573

SUMMARY:
by J Vega-Baudrit · 2024 — This study investigates the perspectives of individuals, primarily Costa Ricans, on the necessity of implementing regulations for nanotechnology.



--------------------------------------------------------------------------------


--- SOURCE 4: The risk perception of nanotechnology: evidence from twitter ---
URL: https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC8989086/

SUMMARY:
by F Murphy · 2022 · Cited by 6 — Debates on nanomaterials' risk tend to be dominated by expert groupings while the general public is largely unaware of the potential hazards.



--------------------------------------------------------------------------------


--- SOURCE 5: Public perception of nanotechnology: A contrast between ... ---
URL: https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S0160791X21002268

SUMMARY:
by A Rathore · 2021 · Cited by 44 — The public generally has a positive attitude towards nanotechnology in developed and developing countries. •. Demographics does not present a consistent picture



--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

URL: https://image.slidesharecdn.com/nanotechnologysafetyconcerns-150415102455-conversion-gate01/75/Nanotechnology-safety-concerns-1-2048.jpg
DESCRIPTION: A presentation slide titled "Nanotechnology – Safety Concerns" for a course by Asadullah Khan, focusing on public attitudes towards nanotechnology safety issues, with the logo of Western Engineering at the bottom left.

IMAGE 4:
URL: https://image.slidesharecdn.com/nanotechnologysafetyconcerns-150415102455-conversion-gate01/95/nanotechnology-safety-concerns-4-638.jpg?cb=1429093581
DESCRIPTION: The content outlines the potential hazards of nanomaterials, particularly focusing on carbon nanotubes, and includes categories such as human health risks, eco-toxicity evaluation, and methodologies for characterization and safety assessment.

IMAGE 5:
URL: https://image.slidesharecdn.com/nanotechnologysafetyconcerns-150415102455-conversion-gate01/85/Nanotechnology-safety-concerns-6-638.jpg
DESCRIPTION: A presentation slide discusses the potential hazards of nanomaterials, highlighting concerns about whether existing tests are sufficient to assess their safety, especially noting that carbon nanotubes may be as dangerous as asbestos.

enriched_points.expanded_content: Research indicates a general positive or neutral perception of nanotechnology among the public, reflecting optimism about its potential benefits (Joubert, 2020).
While general attitudes are positive, variations exist based on demographics, such as socioeconomic status and geographic location, influencing perceptions of nanotech (Rathore, 2021).
Despite positive attitudes, significant concerns remain regarding safety, potential risks, and the need for stringent regulations to govern the use of nanotechnology (Vega-Baudrit, 2024).
